steps.title: Classify the modifier
steps.content: The words before the blank form a complete independent clause: Cahokia had as many as 20,000 inhabitants in 1150 CE. The words after the blank, beginning with making, form a participial phrase that explains the result.

steps.title: Choose the comma
steps.content: A leaves no punctuation before the participial phrase. B turns the participial phrase into a sentence fragment. D uses a semicolon even though making it one of the largest cities is not an independent clause. A comma correctly attaches the phrase to the main clause. The answer is C.
